items.title: NGINX Rift (CVE-2026-42945): 18-Year-Old Heap Overflow Enables Unauthenticated RCE
items.summary: Critical heap buffer overflow in NGINX's ngx_http_rewrite_module (CVSS 9.2) affecting all versions 0.6.27–1.30.0 — virtually every NGINX build since 2008. A size-calculation mismatch in rewrite processing lets an unauthenticated attacker overwrite the heap and achieve RCE via a single crafted HTTP request. A working PoC was published on GitHub by DepthFirst Disclosures; active exploitation confirmed within 3 days of disclosure.
items.why: The affected version range covers millions of internet-facing servers. No authentication required; exploit is one HTTP request; public PoC already exists. Patch to NGINX 1.30.1+ immediately.
items.source: The Hacker News / DepthFirst Disclosures / Akamai Research

items.title: CVE-2026-35616: Fortinet FortiClient EMS Pre-Auth API Bypass (CVSS 9.1) Actively Exploited
items.summary: Improper access control (CWE-284) in FortiClient EMS 7.4.5–7.4.6 lets unauthenticated attackers bypass certificate authentication by spoofing HTTP headers that the Django backend trusts, combined with weak certificate chain validation (string matching only, no cryptographic verification). Attackers can forge certs, gain full authenticated API access, and execute arbitrary commands. CISA added to KEV with a federal remediation deadline.
items.why: FortiClient EMS is the management plane for enterprise endpoint VPN clients — compromise grants control over all managed endpoints. Pre-auth means zero credentials needed, making this a prime ransomware and espionage entry point.
items.source: watchTowr / CISA KEV / The Hacker News

items.title: APT28 Operation Neusploit: Fancy Bear Weaponizes MS Office Zero-Day in <24 Hours, Deploys Cloud C2
items.summary: Russia's APT28 (Fancy Bear) weaponized MS Office CVE-2026-21509 — an OLE security restriction bypass — within 24 hours of disclosure, launching spear-phishing campaigns against EU military, NATO allies, Ukrainian agencies, and maritime/transport orgs across Poland, Slovenia, Turkey, Greece, UAE, and Ukraine. Multi-stage chain deploys NotDoor/MiniDoor Outlook VBA backdoors and a modified Covenant C2 implant ('CovenantGrunt'), abusing filen.io cloud storage as C2 infrastructure to blend with legitimate traffic.
items.why: Sub-24-hour weaponization of a 1-day exploit shows the patch window is effectively zero against nation-state actors. Cloud storage C2 defeats network-level blocking and requires behavioral endpoint detection to catch.
items.source: Zscaler ThreatLabz / Trellix Research / Security Affairs

items.title: Mandiant M-Trends 2026: Attackers Hand Off Access in 22 Seconds; Dwell Time Rises to 14 Days
items.summary: Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 (500,000+ IR hours) finds threat actors are handing off compromised access to secondary operators in as little as 22 seconds via automated credential broker markets. Median dwell time rose to 14 days. Exploits are #1 initial access vector for the sixth consecutive year (32%); voice phishing surged to #2 at 11% of investigations. 714 new malware families tracked in 2025, up from 632, with the total catalog now exceeding 6,000 families. Adversaries increasingly target backup infrastructure to eliminate recovery options and force ransom payment.
items.why: The 22-second hand-off stat breaks traditional SOC response models — hours-long MTTR is now categorically insufficient. Targeting backups directly forces organizations to pay or rebuild entirely, making pre-breach backup isolation critical.

items.title: CVE-2026-44338: PraisonAI AI Agent Framework Auth Bypass Exploited Within 4 Hours of Disclosure
items.summary: PraisonAI versions 2.5.6–4.6.33 shipped its legacy Flask API server with authentication disabled by default, exposing /agents and /chat endpoints to any unauthenticated caller. Attackers can enumerate configured AI agent workflows and trigger arbitrary agent executions without credentials. First malicious scanner probing this CVE appeared 3 hours 44 minutes after the advisory was published on May 11, 2026. A related critical RCE flaw (CVE-2026-40287) is also documented. Fixed in PraisonAI 4.6.34.
items.why: AI agent frameworks are an emerging, under-scrutinized attack surface. Agents configured with broad system access (file I/O, code execution, API calls) mean unauthenticated /chat access can translate to full RCE. The 4-hour exploitation onset signals attackers are actively hunting AI infrastructure.
